title: World Religions
description: Major world religions, sacred texts, key figures, and practices

cards.front: What is the sacred text of Christianity?
cards.back: The Bible
Sacred text of Christianity

cards.front: Who is the central figure of Christianity?
cards.back: Jesus Christ
A set of biblical laws given to Moses on Mount Sinai, central to Judaism and Christianity

cards.front: What is the Christian doctrine of the Trinity?
cards.back: The belief that God exists as three persons: the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit

cards.front: What are the three major branches of Christianity?
cards.back: Roman Catholic, Eastern Orthodox, and Protestant
A Christian sacrament of admission into the faith, using water

cards.front: What Christian holiday celebrates the resurrection of Jesus?
cards.back: Easter

cards.front: What is the sacred text of Islam?
cards.back: The Quran
Sacred text of Islam

cards.front: Who is the founder and chief prophet of Islam?
cards.back: Muhammad
Founder and chief prophet of Islam

cards.front: What are the Five Pillars of Islam?
cards.back: Shahada (faith), Salat (prayer), Zakat (charity), Sawm (fasting), and Hajj (pilgrimage)
The holiest city in Islam, located in Saudi Arabia; birthplace of Muhammad
An Islamic place of worship
The ninth month of the Islamic calendar, observed by Muslims as a month of fasting and prayer
The annual Islamic pilgrimage to Mecca, one of the Five Pillars of Islam

cards.front: What is the sacred text of Judaism?
cards.back: The Torah
Sacred text of Judaism (the first five books of the Hebrew Bible)
A central text of Judaism containing rabbinical discussions on law, ethics, and customs
A Jewish place of worship
A patriarch considered the founding father of Judaism, Christianity, and Islam

cards.front: Patriarch considered the founding father of Judaism, Christianity, and Islam
cards.back: Abraham
The prophet who led the Israelites out of Egypt and received the Ten Commandments

cards.front: Prophet who led the Israelites out of Egypt
cards.back: Moses
A Jewish holiday commemorating the Israelites' liberation from slavery in Egypt
An eight-day Jewish festival commemorating the rededication of the Second Temple in Jerusalem

cards.front: What is a Bar Mitzvah (or Bat Mitzvah)?
cards.back: A Jewish coming-of-age ceremony at age 13 (boys) or 12–13 (girls)
The oldest and most sacred scriptures of Hinduism
The oldest and most sacred scriptures of Hinduism
A sacred Hindu scripture that is a dialogue between Prince Arjuna and the god Krishna
A sacred Hindu scripture; a dialogue between Prince Arjuna and the god Krishna
The Hindu god of creation, part of the Trimurti
The Hindu god of preservation, part of the Trimurti
The Hindu god of destruction and transformation, part of the Trimurti
The three main gods of Hinduism: Brahma (creator), Vishnu (preserver), and Shiva (destroyer)
The principle that a person's actions in this life determine their fate in future lives (Hinduism and Buddhism)
One's moral duty or righteous path in life
The belief that the soul is reborn into a new body after death (central to Hinduism and Buddhism)
The Hindu festival of lights, celebrating the victory of light over darkness
Siddhartha Gautama (the Buddha)
The founder of Buddhism, known as the Buddha (the Enlightened One)

cards.front: What are the Four Noble Truths of Buddhism?
cards.back: Life involves suffering; suffering is caused by desire; suffering can end; the way to end it is the Eightfold Path
The Buddhist path to end suffering: right view, intention, speech, action, livelihood, effort, mindfulness, and concentration
The ultimate goal of Buddhism — liberation from the cycle of death and rebirth, and the end of suffering

cards.front: What is the sangha in Buddhism?
cards.back: The community of Buddhist monks, nuns, and practitioners
Guru Nanak
The founder of Sikhism (born 1469)

cards.front: What is the Guru Granth Sahib?
cards.back: The sacred scripture of Sikhism, treated as a living guru
The sacred scripture of Sikhism
Confucius (Kong Qiu)
Chinese philosopher and founder of Confucianism (551–479 BCE)

cards.front: What is the central text of Confucianism?
cards.back: The Analects
The central text of Confucianism, a collection of sayings by Confucius

cards.front: Who is the traditional founder of Taoism?
cards.back: Laozi (Lao Tzu)
Traditional founder of Taoism and author of the Tao Te Ching

cards.front: What is the Tao Te Ching?
cards.back: The foundational text of Taoism, attributed to Laozi

cards.front: What is the largest religion in the world by number of followers?
cards.back: Christianity

cards.front: What is the second-largest religion in the world by number of followers?
cards.back: Islam

cards.front: What term describes Judaism, Christianity, and Islam as a group?
cards.back: Abrahamic religions (they share Abraham as a common patriarch)
